source: Structured Interview - BWI - Senior Management Capabilities.docx
note: Curated question->subtopic backfill. question = interview question from the doc (blank where the competency is the interviewer's end-of-section judgement, not a distinct question). purpose = the doc's 'Check:' guidance / judgement line.

Subtopic01.1.question: Tell us a bit more about your role and your organizational structure within BWI.
Subtopic01.1.purpose: Understanding of BWI's culture and how their role fits the wider organization.

Subtopic01.2.purpose: Is this person an open and clear communicator? Closed and defensive (low) vs open and cooperative (high).

Subtopic02.1.question: Tell me about the competition that this facility faces over the next 2 years?
Subtopic02.1.purpose: Clear understanding of the organization, business environment and competitive landscape — good knowledge vs evasive & shallow.

Subtopic02.2.purpose: Ability to translate and apply strategies on a day-to-day basis — pro-active and clear thinking vs messy answers.

Subtopic02.3.purpose: Does he/she think strategically (high), tactically (mid), or is a follower (low)?

Subtopic03.1.question: Please give an example where other Departments assist you and your team in achieving your strategic goals?
Subtopic03.1.purpose: Silo mentality vs genuine interest in wider group strategy.

Subtopic03.2.question: How do you check your direct reports are delivering their goals/responsibilities?
Subtopic03.2.purpose: Ability to delegate, provide encouragement and support.

Subtopic03.3.purpose: Does he/she accept responsibility and full accountability across silos (high), or focus only on own silo with minimal ownership outside (low)?

Subtopic04.1.question: When someone reports a possible product quality risk, what do you do?
Subtopic04.1.purpose: Is there initial investigation before escalation? Think and take ownership (high) vs pass to others with minimal ownership (low).

Subtopic04.2.purpose: Their support to the person reporting the quality issue — blame the messenger (low) vs take personal responsibility and thank them (high).

Subtopic04.3.purpose: Does he/she accept responsibility and full accountability easily but with care (high), indifferent (mid), or evasive (low)?

Subtopic05.1.question: Who has been your best boss? What was it about his/her management style that brought the best out in you?
Subtopic05.1.purpose: Assess the level of autonomy sought and granted.

Subtopic05.2.question: If something major goes wrong, when and how will you let your boss know?
Subtopic05.2.purpose: Whether they communicate with integrity and acknowledge mistakes.

Subtopic05.3.purpose: Does he/she communicate with passion and persuasiveness (high), or too complicated and without personal commitment (low)?

Subtopic06.1.question: Give me an example when you challenged the historic ways things were done and had to build a case for change.
Subtopic06.1.purpose: The extent of innovative and unconventional ideas that add value to the company.

Subtopic06.2.question: What are the initial things that you expect BWI to do?
Subtopic06.2.purpose: Insights vs indifference, and whether their ideas are positive.

Subtopic06.3.purpose: Is he/she committed to change in an informed manner (high), indifferent (mid), or resisting (low)?

Subtopic07.1.question: Who sets your quarterly/monthly operational performance goals, and how do you ensure your team's daily activities deliver to your quarterly objectives?
Subtopic07.1.purpose: Ability to stay focused and constructively address emotions and conflicts — caught in micromanagement (low) vs oversee and analyse the issues to be addressed (high).

Subtopic07.2.purpose: Is he/she persuasive and positive (high), dull and negative (mid), or indifferent (low)?

Subtopic07.3.purpose: Is he/she seeking a win-win situation through a give-and-take process?

Subtopic08.1.question: How do you encourage your most senior subordinate to apply lean behaviours in the teams?
Subtopic08.1.purpose: Ability to use quantitative and qualitative KPIs to measure performance — clarity and numeracy (high) vs words without calibration (low).

Subtopic08.2.question: What are the most important aspects of Lean Management that you follow daily?
Subtopic08.2.purpose: The depth of understanding of Lean.

Subtopic08.3.purpose: Is he/she genuinely engaged in applying Lean Management principles on a disciplined daily basis?

Subtopic09.1.question: With addressing new initiatives, how do you determine who should be part of the decision-making process?
Subtopic09.1.purpose: Ability to identify the relevant stakeholders to get consensus (high) without involving everyone to cover their backs (low).

Subtopic09.2.question: How do you make a decision when you don't have all the information you need (uncertainties and ambiguities), yet you still need to decide?
Subtopic09.2.purpose: Ability to manage ambiguity and stay focused on the 'big picture', identifying patterns and connections (high) vs seeking more and more data to protect their back (low).

Subtopic09.3.purpose: Is this person a decisive decision maker?

Subtopic10.1.question: What do you do when a major project that you are running starts to run behind schedule?
Subtopic10.1.purpose: Ability to respond quickly, interpret conflicting information and prioritise (high) vs seeking others to become involved to make the decisions (low).

Subtopic10.2.purpose: Is he/she very aware of the importance of on-time delivery (high), aware but views risk as not their responsibility (mid), or indifferent (low)?

Subtopic11.1.question: What do you do if your suggestion for improvement of the operational processes is rejected?
Subtopic11.1.purpose: Ability to handle rejection and re-try later (high) vs acceptance (low).

Subtopic11.2.purpose: Will this person be a unifying and stable member of the management team (high), or divisive and evasive (low)?

Subtopic11.3.purpose: Does this person have inner strength (does not need outside affirmation)?

Subtopic12.1.question: Please think for a moment, then give me 2 to 3 messages that you would like us to carry back to the Senior Managers of BWI. (Confidential comments welcome.)
Subtopic12.1.purpose: Assess the depth and level of concern: high concern and frustration (high), indifferent (mid), no fears (low).

Subtopic12.2.purpose: Assess the depth and level of concern: high concern and frustration (high), indifferent (mid), no fears (low).

Subtopic12.3.purpose: Assess the depth and level of concern: high concern and frustration (high), indifferent (mid), no fears (low).
